hot_topics.synthesis: The US and Iran have signed a preliminary 14-point memorandum of understanding to end the war, which includes an end to fighting, a pledge for Iran to never possess nuclear weapons, and a $300 billion redevelopment package for Iran. While some see the deal as a surrender, others believe careful enforcement could achieve US aims, with debates ongoing about its effectiveness and the specifics of its terms, especially regarding nuclear ambitions and regional stability. The agreement has drawn criticism from both sides of the political spectrum regarding its implications for American foreign policy and Iran's position.

AI technology continues rapid development, with new large language models like GLM-5.2 showing impressive coding and general intelligence capabilities. Adobe is integrating AI assistants into its Creative Cloud apps like Photoshop and Premiere to help with organization and task automation, and is also reimagining its Firefly AI studio for more persistent context in design. However, the AI boom also presents challenges, including the geopolitical concern that world leaders fear losing access to American AI and the impact on hardware costs for devices.

Ukraine launched a significant wave of drone attacks on Russia, hitting an oil refinery near Moscow and other sites, leading to reports of black rain from residents. This large-scale assault, one of the biggest on the Russian capital since the invasion, raises questions about Russia's response and whether President Putin will alter his military tactics in the conflict.

hot_topics.topic: Apple Product Prices Rising Due to AI Costs
hot_topics.synthesis: The rising costs of AI, particularly for chips, are projected to force Apple to increase prices for its products, including iPhones. CEO Tim Cook acknowledged that the current situation is unsustainable, indicating future price adjustments without specifying affected products or timing.

items.title: Neanderthal 1
items.summary: The story of 16 Neanderthal bones discovered in Germany is highlighted as having reshaped our understanding of early human species and ourselves. The site where these bones were found became crucial for comprehending Neanderthals.

items.title: Words, words, words
items.summary: An essay argues that literature enthusiasts should embrace AI as a fellow wordsmith, despite strong resistance from writers. The author suggests focusing on the shared foundation of language between humans and machines.

items.title: "Truly evil" FDA rejection of gene therapy overturned after Trump official ousted
items.summary: A gene therapy for Huntington's disease, AMT-130 by UniQure, has received a new path towards FDA approval after the reversal of a previous rejection. The initial "truly evil" rejection, overseen by an ousted Trump official, had blocked a promising treatment showing potential to slow disease progression.

items.title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
items.summary: What happens when you give AI coding agents a lab full of robotic arms, some compute resources, and a “generous token budget” for teaching the robots various tasks? The agents can apparently figure out a training regimen that teaches the robots to successfully cut zip ties and ev…

items.title: California says AT&T lied to FCC in attempt to shut off old phone network
items.summary: California regulators accuse AT&T of lying to the FCC in an attempt to shut down its old copper phone network without providing an adequate replacement. AT&T is also suing California over its refusal to allow the company to cease service to approximately 199,000 wireline customers.

items.title: Hulk, Punisher join Peter Parker in Spider-Man: Brand New Day trailer
items.summary: Sony Pictures released a new trailer for "Spider-Man: Brand New Day," the sequel to "No Way Home," set to debut in about six weeks. The film continues Peter Parker's story after he asked Doctor Strange to erase his memory from everyone, including MJ, to protect the multiverse.

items.title: Hunter-gatherers in Siberia died of a plague outbreak 5,500 years ago
items.summary: Ancient DNA research confirmed a plague outbreak 5,500 years ago among Siberian hunter-gatherers, identifying the earliest known strain of Yersinia pestis. This discovery challenges previous theories that the plague only became lethal with denser agricultural settlements.

items.title: Second carcass-eating fly species cleared by FDA for maggot wound therapy
items.summary: The FDA has cleared a second fly species, Lucilia cuprina, for maggot wound therapy, potentially expanding the biosurgery market. Cuprina Holdings now has FDA clearance for two species, including the commonly used Lucilia sericata.

items.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
items.summary: Amazon and Quera promise useful quantum error correction as early as 2028, a timeframe considered remarkably soon by many in the field. This advancement is crucial for applying quantum computing to complex problems, requiring logical qubits for error detection and correction.

items.title: Ten months later, the $100 Google Home Speaker is finally available for preorder
items.summary: Google's new Home Speaker, its first smart home audio device in nearly six years, is finally available for preorder at $100 after being announced last August. The small, round, fabric-covered speaker offers "360-degree sound," features a light ring, and includes three far-field microphones.

items.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
items.summary: UK retail conglomerate Tesco is migrating 40,000 server workloads off VMware, citing "abusive conduct" from Broadcom after its acquisition of VMware. Tesco alleges Broadcom refused to honor previous licensing deals and demanded inflated prices for virtualization software already paid for.

items.title: The Slate Truck's price may have leaked, starts at $24,950
items.summary: The price for Slate Auto's much-anticipated Blank Slate electric pickup truck may have leaked, reportedly starting at $24,950. The vehicle is designed as a back-to-basics EV with a compact footprint and optional features, targeting affordability after the $7,500 IRS clean vehicle tax credit was abolished.

items.title: Waiting For The Miracle
items.summary: The author investigated recurring 'sun miracles' witnessed by thousands of people in Fatima, Portugal, and Medjugorje, Bosnia, and similar events, which defy conventional explanations. This personal exploration seeks to understand phenomena often dismissed as mass hallucinations.

items.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
items.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

items.title: 'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
items.summary: A growing backlash against delivery robots is emerging in US cities, with bans and protest groups forming as these vehicles increasingly operate on streets. Concerns are rising about their impact on public spaces and pedestrian safety.

items.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
items.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

items.title: Bird flu kills more than 75% of baby seals on remote Australian island, study finds
items.summary: A study found that bird flu (H5N1 strain) has killed over 75% of baby seals on remote Heard Island, with scientists reporting 13,000 southern elephant seal pups dead. This outbreak highlights a significant wildlife health crisis.

items.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
items.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

items.title: British man dies in paragliding accident in Spain
items.summary: A 63-year-old British man died in a paragliding accident in the Palau de Noguera area of Catalonia, Spain. Authorities confirmed his death on Wednesday.

items.title: First Russian shadow fleet tanker enters Channel since Smyrtos boarding
items.summary: The Forwarder, a Russian-flagged vessel from the 'shadow fleet,' entered the Channel on Wednesday evening, marking the first time such a tanker has done so since the Smyrtos boarding incident. This movement signals continued Russian maritime activity despite international scrutiny.

items.title: Gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport for second time in months
items.summary: Gunmen attacked Niger's largest airport for the second time in months, continuing a decade-long militant Islamist insurgency in the country. Suspected jihadists also targeted the same airport in January.

items.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
items.summary: US Defense Secretary Hegseth has renewed criticism of NATO and indicated that the US will review its military presence in Europe. This follows a previous decision by the US to scale back its commitments to the alliance's high readiness force.

items.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
items.summary: Japan's Defence Minister Koizumi stated that ramping up defense is 'critical' to prevent war, signaling a need for Japan to re-evaluate its post-World War Two pacifist stance. This move is aimed at strengthening the country's security posture.

items.title: Jury fails to reach verdict in Norwegian 'hitman' trial
items.summary: A jury failed to reach a verdict in the trial of Johannes Natland, 19, who was accused in a Norwegian 'hitman' case. Natland was remanded in custody and will face a retrial.

items.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
items.summary: A lawyer involved in a high-profile Ugandan treason case has been charged with a related offense, following claims by Uganda's military chief of inflicting 'hurt and pain' on the arrested lawyer. The situation escalates political tensions around the case.

items.title: Lil Nas X says he's 'feeling better' after rehab and bipolar diagnosis
items.summary: Lil Nas X announced he is 'feeling better' after completing rehab and receiving a bipolar diagnosis. The artist had been arrested last year for attacking police officers before entering treatment.

items.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles
items.summary: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
items.summary: A debate is ongoing in the US regarding whether to implement a social media ban for teenagers, similar to a UK policy restricting access for those under 16. Americans of various ages were asked for their opinions on the potential US ban.

items.title: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
items.summary: A suspected gang leader was shot dead in a flower bouquet ambush at Guayaquil airport, as gunmen concealed weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys. The incident occurred while the victim was waiting outside the airport.

items.title: Teenager dies in horse-drawn carriage accident in New York
items.summary: A teenager died in a horse-drawn carriage accident in New York after the horse bolted and toppled over. The incident occurred when the carriage driver stepped down to take a picture of the passengers.

items.title: The Ring and Lilo & Stitch actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
items.summary: Actress Daveigh Chase, known for 'The Ring' and 'Lilo & Stitch,' died at age 35 from sepsis following a bout with meningitis. Her longtime manager confirmed her passing in a Los Angeles hospital.

items.title: Trump says he will visit India as frosty relationship with Modi thaws
items.summary: Trump made the announcement during chat where he also vowed to protect India.

items.title: UN experts demand Iranians free Foremans
items.summary: UN experts are demanding Iran release Lindsay and Craig Foreman, stating that proceedings against them have been marred by grave irregularities. The call highlights concerns over human rights and legal due process in Iran.

items.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
items.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

items.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
items.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

items.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
items.summary: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

items.title: Why Iran believes deal with US leaves it stronger than before
items.summary: Iran has come out of the war's first chapter stronger than many expected, but its next challenge may be harder, the BBC Persian Service's Amir Azimi writes.

items.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
items.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

items.summary:

items.title: Emacs 31 is around the corner: The changes I'm daily driving
items.summary: Emacs 31 is nearing release, with a developer detailing the changes they frequently use in the upcoming version. The update promises new features and improvements for users of the popular text editor.

items.title: Hospitals and universities repurposing drugs at 90% lower cost
items.summary: Hospitals and universities are successfully repurposing existing drugs at a reported 90% lower cost, offering a more affordable approach to treatment development. This strategy leverages known compounds for new medical applications.

items.title: I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
items.summary: A security researcher discovered over 10,000 GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware. This widespread distribution poses a significant threat to developers and users who rely on open-source code.

items.title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
items.summary:
Article URL:
https://blog.alexellis.io/local-ai-is-not-opus/
Comments URL:

items.title: Microsoft new Outlook takes 10 seconds to do what Outlook Classic does instantly
items.summary: Users are reporting that Microsoft's new Outlook application takes significantly longer to perform tasks compared to the classic Outlook. This performance issue causes frustration for those accustomed to the instant responsiveness of the older version.

items.summary:

items.title: Swiss parliament lifts ban on new nuclear power plants
items.summary: The Swiss parliament has voted to lift the ban on new nuclear power plants, marking a significant shift in the country's energy policy. This decision could pave the way for future nuclear energy development.

items.title: As America turns 250, one museum makes history possible to touch
items.summary: A museum is making history accessible to blind visitors by allowing them to touch artifacts, challenging federal laws that require accessibility but often limit interaction with exhibits. This initiative aims to improve access for people with disabilities as America approaches its 250th anniversary.

items.title: Embedded: "We Keep Us Safe" from NPR, KUOW and The Seattle Times
items.summary: In summer 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. was shot and killed in Seattle's Capitol Hill Occupied Protest (CHOP) zone, a case that remains unsolved. This report from NPR, KUOW, and The Seattle Times delves into the events surrounding his death.

items.title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war
items.summary:
Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.
(Image credit: Nathan Laine)

items.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
items.summary: A new report by MIT researchers suggests Russia's nuclear-powered Burevestnik missile likely flies by heating air with a nuclear reactor, calling it 'almost certainly a terrible idea.' Analysts confirm the concept is feasible but extremely dangerous.

items.title: Supreme Court sides with a marijuana user who was barred from owning guns
items.summary:
The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.
(Image credit: Drew Angerer)

items.title: The Obama Presidential Center will be dedicated Thursday. Here's what to expect
items.summary: The Obama Presidential Center is set to open with a star-studded dedication ceremony, showcasing the former president's legacy through its museum. The center, however, is not a traditional presidential library.

items.title: These 3 brothers lost their parents to AIDS. Now they struggle to make it on their own
items.summary: Three brothers are navigating life independently after losing their parents to AIDS due to a lack of access to HIV medications. Their story highlights the ongoing challenges faced by child-headed families and HIV orphans.

items.title: Trump signs agreement with Iran. And, the president's approval hits record lows
items.summary:
Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.
(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)

items.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
items.summary:
Ukraine launched a new wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday, amounting to one of the largest attacks on the Russian capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
(Image credit: AFP)

items.title: Kenyan McDuffie concedes to Janeese Lewis George in DC mayoral primary
items.summary: Janeese Lewis George is expected to become Washington D.C.'s next mayor after Kenyan McDuffie conceded the Democratic primary. Lewis George, a progressive, campaigned on opposing President Trump's influence on the district and plans to reform public safety and housing.

items.title: Why the Human Genome’s Tangled Physicality May Confound AI
items.summary: Since its molecular structure was deduced in the 1950s, DNA has been hailed by many biologists as the secret of life. They’ve read and studied the information stored in the DNA found in the cells of living organisms, known as their genomes, and claimed that this genetic database…

items.summary:
What happened in 2025 was this:
the economics of code production were turned upside down
. Instead of being very hard, time-consuming, and expensive to generate code, it became effectively free and instant. Lines of code went from being treasured, reused, cared for and carefully…

items.title: Congress cuts a deal on the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act
items.summary: Congress reached a bipartisan agreement on the final version of the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act after months of negotiations. The bill aims to address housing challenges, with its details to be further examined.

items.title: A tech worker-backed PAC is bringing a $5M knife to Big Tech’s $100M gunfight
items.summary: Guardrails positions itself as a populist political movement that runs on small donations from people in the trenches of the AI boom.

items.title: Adobe adds its AI assistant to Premiere, Illustrator and InDesign
items.summary: Adobe is updating its Firefly AI assistant with new chops, and adding it to Premiere, Illustrator, InDesign and Frame.io.

items.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
items.summary: Snap's stock declined after the unveiling of its "ridiculously expensive" augmented reality glasses. The highly anticipated smart glasses debut did not generate a positive market reaction for the company.

items.title: AI is hurting Apple in more ways than one: it may force iPhone price increases
items.summary: CEO Tim Cook said in a recent interview that the situation is "unsustainable."

items.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
items.summary: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

items.title: Apple opens up App Store to new competition in Brazil
items.summary: Apple is relaxing its control over iPhone app distribution in Brazil, opening its App Store to new competition in that major market. This move could change how users access and developers distribute applications in the region.

items.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI
items.summary: Chi-Hua Chien has spent more than two decades as a venture capitalist, but he thinks like a cultural anthropologist.

items.title: Cybercriminals allegedly hacked tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies all over the world
items.summary: Cybercriminals, reportedly a Russian-speaking group, have allegedly compromised tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies globally. They exploited previously known vulnerabilities in Fortinet VPNs and firewalls.

items.title: FTC lawsuit reveals how subscription scam networks evade app store enforcement
items.summary: An FTC lawsuit has exposed how sophisticated subscription app networks allegedly use shell companies and payment infrastructure to evade app store enforcement despite numerous consumer complaints. This reveals tactics used to maintain fraudulent operations on app platforms.

items.title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
items.summary: The startup trains embodied AI and world models using Medal’s dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million monthly active users.

items.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
items.summary: Here's what you need to do to get those pesky "write with Gemini" pop-ups to go away.

items.title: MapTap, a daily geography game, is my new Wordle
items.summary: MapTap, a daily geography game, offers an engaging way to feel smarter about world locations. This phone game provides an interactive experience for users to test their geographical knowledge.

items.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
items.summary: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts…

items.title: Pixi’s new iOS app turns text messages into interactive AR experiences
items.summary: Pixi's new iOS app transforms text messages into interactive augmented reality (AR) experiences, moving beyond traditional stickers and GIFs. The company believes AR is the next evolution for digital messaging.

items.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
items.summary: Social media platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok are evolving towards user-controlled algorithms, allowing greater customization of feeds. This shift gives users direct influence over their content recommendations.

items.title: Spotify’s reserved ticket sales to music superfans are now going live
items.summary: Spotify is launching "Reserved," a new program that offers two concert tickets to an artist's superfans before public sale. This initiative aims to reward dedicated listeners with early access to live music events.

items.title: Waymo recalls nearly 4,000 robotaxis to stop them driving into highway construction zones
items.summary: Waymo has recalled nearly 4,000 robotaxis to prevent them from driving into highway construction zones after identifying 13 such instances. This recall addresses a software issue causing autonomous vehicles to enter closed sections of highways.

items.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
items.summary: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight  — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

items.summary:

This question comes amid rumors that a fo…

items.title: Tough Love: My Father-in-Law Is Spoiled
items.summary: An advice column addresses a reader's frustration with his father-in-law's spoiled behavior, where he expects his wife to cater to his every need. The columnist advises accepting that individual marriages are idiosyncratic and not for outsiders to 'fix.'

items.summary:

uncon…

items.title: Why Syria Isn’t the Answer to Lebanon’s Misery
items.summary: Negotiations to end the US-Iran war have prompted discussions about Syria’s potential role in addressing Hezbollah in Lebanon. While Syria's current government is no ally of Hezbollah, President Trump's suggestion of a 'surgical attack' by Syria is met with skepticism regarding its realism and implications for regional stability.

items.title: Adobe’s redesigned AI studio remembers what your creations look like
items.summary:
Give your characters, objects, and backgrounds a name to easily replicate them without changing the design. | Image: Adobe
Adobe is introducing some new capabilities for its Firefly AI assistant, alongside a "reimagined" AI studio that lets you edit and generate new designs from…

items.title: Amazon employees say they’re facing termination for backing data center limits
items.summary: Three Amazon software engineers accuse the company of retaliating against them, alleging potential termination, after they testified at a Seattle City Council hearing supporting data center limits. The employees claim Amazon's "Employee Relations" began investigating them shortly after the City Council passed a data center moratorium.

items.title: Photoshop and Premiere now have AI assistants
items.summary:
﻿Photoshop is one of several Adobe Creative Cloud apps to receive new conversational editing capabilities. | Image: Adobe
Adobe's plan to stick AI assistants into all of its Creative Cloud suite is now fully underway, with new chatbots now rolling out to its biggest editing and d…
